name: Club Nautico de Camarinas
approachNotes: Inside Ria de Camarinas, S of Cabo Vilan. Two channels: Canal de las Quebrantas (N - keep clear of Bajo Bufardo at Cabo Vilan; hermitage on 135 then 179 with Muxia church, pass between Farelo and Quebranta Chica) or Canal de A Barca (S, wider/easier). Call VHF 09. Some ground swell in strong W.
nearestTown: Camarinas village 5 min; supermarkets, pharmacy; 15 km from Malpica.
orcaNotes: Offshore Cabo Vilan/Touriñan in active GTOA zone (2020-2025). Stay inside 20 m contour; Fisterra Radio Ch 16 for advisory.
notes: Only year-round marina on the Costa da Morte (Blue Flag). Essential refuge between La Coruña and Fisterra. Sailing school.

name: Marina Muxia
approachNotes: S shore of Ria de Camarinas. Canal A Barca (S, wider - Punta Villueira on 082 then 179 with Muxia church) or Canal Quebrantas (N - avoid Bajo Bufardo, 135 to hermitage then 179). Hazards: Las Quebrantas and Leixon de Juanboy shoals. Limited fuel-dock manoeuvring room.
nearestTown: Muxia village 5 min (Camino pilgrim destination); good seafood.
orcaNotes: Cabo Touriñan (just S) is a documented interaction zone; treat rounding as high risk. Report to Fisterra Radio Ch 16.
notes: Modern marina (2018), Blue Flag; one of the best-equipped on Costa da Morte. Camino waypoint.

name: Porto de Fisterra (Finisterre)
approachNotes: Small fishing harbour on the S side of Cabo Fisterra. Protected by main + SE breakwaters. Approach from NE after rounding the cape - keep well off (above-water rocks, kelp). In NW conditions prefer anchoring in Ria de Corcubion to the S. Inner harbour fills with fishing boats. Swell rolls in during southerlies.
nearestTown: Fisterra town (immediate); pilgrim infrastructure; limited provisioning.
orcaNotes: Cape Finisterre is ground zero for Iberian orca interactions. Contact Fisterra Radio Ch 16 before offshore passage; stay inside 20 m contour. Incidents to 8 nm off the cape; orcas entering ria margins in late summer/autumn.
notes: Major psychological waypoint ('Land's End'). Fishing-harbour character, not full-service. Ria de Corcubion is a better refuge in most conditions.

name: Corcubion / Cee (Ria de Corcubion anchorage)
approachNotes: Ria opens E of Cabo Fisterra, wide and well marked. After rounding the cape keep 1+ nm off to clear rocks. Anchor off Corcubion in 5-8 m sand/mud, good holding, sheltered from N/NW. Small dinghy pontoon. Cee (1 km) has a large supermarket.
swellSensitivity: low in NW-N; open to E/SE winds
nearestTown: Corcubion (restaurants, fish shop); Cee 1 km (large supermarket); Santiago 80 km.
orcaNotes: Inside ria low risk (shallow); offshore cape area high - see Fisterra.
notes: Classic refuge after rounding Cabo Fisterra; excellent shelter from prevailing NW summer winds.

name: Marina Muros (Muport)
region: Galicia / Ria de Muros e Noia
approachNotes: Ria de Muros ~3 nm wide at the mouth - approach unproblematic day or night. NE course; after Cabo Rebordino light turn into Ensenada de Muros. Marina behind breakwater in outer basin. Call VHF 09. Watch lobster pots near N entrance; NE swell brings short chop.
nearestTown: Muros (immediate) - medieval arcaded town, fish market; Santiago 55 km.
orcaNotes: Well inside ria - negligible. Offshore from Fisterra area remains medium.
notes: Gated modern marina in a striking medieval town. ASNAUGA discount.

name: Real Club Nautico Portosin
region: Galicia / Ria de Muros e Noia
approachNotes: S side of Ria de Muros e Noia. Mid-channel, avoid Quiebra island; 160 m canal between N end of fishing pier and dock light to E. Inner depth 6.4 m; mouth 8.5 m. Blue Flag.
nearestTown: Portosin hamlet (bar, shop); Porto do Son 3 km (supermarket delivers to boats for orders >EUR50); Santiago 40 km (nearest airport).
orcaNotes: Deep inside ria - negligible. Offshore Cabo Corrubedo (S) medium.
notes: Widely regarded the finest marina in Ria de Muros. 34 t travelift; English-speaking staff. 25% CA/ICC/RCC/OCC discount.

name: Club Nautico Deportivo de Ribeira
approachNotes: SW corner of Ria de Arousa, N of Punta Cabeiro. Mouth 5 m, inner 2 m - shallow for deeper draught. Entrance 140 m wide. Watch Rua islet (mid-ria, small lighthouse) - navigate the channel S of it. Heavy fishing traffic.
nearestTown: Ribeira (immediate) - large fishing town, full provisioning, banks, pharmacy.
orcaNotes: Well inside ria. Isla Salvora at ria entrance medium for offshore.
notes: Busy fishing port; small yacht-club marina (local boats <=14 m). 40 t travelift.
verifyNeeded: maxDraft - inner 2 m hard limit at MLW

name: Puerto de Aguino
approachNotes: NW entrance to Ria de Arousa. 300 m breakwater. Access via N channel between Isla de Salvora and mainland - identify Forcadiñas stones, Arbelas rocks, Meixon Vigo shoal. Primarily fishing; yacht access only in settled weather. Canal de Sagres offshore is challenging (250 m wide, 7-10 m) - calm + good charts only.
nearestTown: Aguino fishing village; Ribeira 5 km.
orcaNotes: Offshore from Isla de Salvora / N entrance of Arousa is an orca transition zone; Canal de Sagres route carries risk.
notes: Emergency refuge only - fishing port, no yacht marina.

name: Club Nautico do Caraminal (A Pobra do Caraminal)
approachNotes: N coast of Ria de Arousa between Punta de Ladiña and Punta de la Merced. Main channel S of Jidoiro Pedregoso and Rua islet (1600 m wide, 60-70 m deep). Inner 3 m, entrance 5 m. Watch mussel rafts (bateas) throughout Arousa.
nearestTown: A Pobra do Caraminal (immediate) - large Gadis supermarket, beach.
orcaNotes: Well inside ria - negligible.
notes: Well-sheltered marina in a pleasant town. 40 t travelift. Muport sister marina here has fuel. Bus to Santiago.
verifyNeeded: fuel at club berths (Muport Pobra has fuel)

name: Marina Vilagarcia de Arousa
approachNotes: SE corner of Ria de Arousa via main channel - beware numerous UNLIT mussel rafts (bateas), alert navigation at night/fog. Entrance 25 m wide, 5 m at entrance, 3 m inner. Integrated in commercial port. VHF 09.
bestTideEntry: check depth for >2.5 m draft at MLW
nearestTown: Vilagarcia (immediate) - city, full provisioning, RAIL station (Santiago 40 min), hospitals.
orcaNotes: Fully inside ria.
notes: Largest marina in the ria; rail link makes it an excellent crew-change port. 50 t travelift. ASNAUGA.

name: Marina Arousa (Vilanova de Arousa)
approachNotes: Mid-ria at Vilanova de Arousa via main channel avoiding bateas. Entrance 50 m wide, 5 m at mouth, 3 m inside. Mussel-raft anchor chains hang vertically - can foul props if passing close.
nearestTown: Vilanova de Arousa 5 min; supermarkets, bakeries.
orcaNotes: Fully inside ria.
notes: Recently built marina; 35 t travelift, good workshop; quieter alternative to Vilagarcia.

name: Cambados (anchorage / fishing port)
approachNotes: Extremely shallow (<2 m); many shoals/banks to Cambados and Tragove/O Grove - careful chart work. Anchor out in deeper water to SE and use dinghy. No sports marina (planned but unbuilt). Approach near HW only for any keeled yacht.
bestTideEntry: near HW only for keeled yachts
nearestTown: Cambados (dinghy) - Albariño wine capital, arcaded plaza, August wine festival.
orcaNotes: Fully inside ria.
notes: Worth a dinghy visit for Albariño and the medieval town; not a marina stop for keel yachts except at HW.

name: Club Nautico San Vicente do Mar (O Grove)
approachNotes: Porto Pedras Negras at the tip of the O Grove peninsula. Approach from E via marked channel - tricky in strong wind. Entrance 90 m, mouth 5 m, inner 2 m - shallow; keeled yachts near HW. Avoid O Grove shoals W of peninsula. 32 t travelift.
nearestTown: O Grove 10 min - seafood town, pulperia, beach.
orcaNotes: Well inside ria.
notes: Base for O Grove peninsula and Illa de Ons day trip. Small-medium yachts only (14 m, 2 m draft).

name: Nauta Sanxenxo (Puerto Deportivo Juan Carlos I)
approachNotes: N shore of Ria de Pontevedra. Approach from W between Illa de Ons/Onza (swell protection) and mainland. Punta Cabicastro (44 m monolith) key landmark; Helmo rock 100 m S of it - give clearance. Entrance 70 m, mouth 7.5 m, inner 3.5 m.
swellSensitivity: low-moderate; Ons gives protection but strong SW swell can enter
nearestTown: Sanxenxo (immediate) - busy beach resort, full services; Pontevedra 15 km.
orcaNotes: Inside ria low; offshore past Cabo Udra (NW) medium in summer/autumn.
notes: Largest marina in Ria de Pontevedra (to 45 m). 64 t travelift. Very busy Jul/Aug.

name: Club Nautico de Portonovo
approachNotes: 1 nm W of Sanxenxo; same ria approach (clear of Helmo rock S of Cabicastro). Entrance 170 m, mouth 8 m, inner 2 m. 100 t travelift.
bestTideEntry: inner 2 m at LW - check draft
nearestTown: Portonovo (quieter than Sanxenxo); Sanxenxo 1 km.
orcaNotes: Inside ria; same offshore caveat as Sanxenxo.
notes: Smaller, well-equipped; 100 t travelift excellent for haulout. Better for boats under 10-16 m.

name: Illa de Ons - anchorage
approachNotes: Two designated anchorage zones only: CASTELO (NE) and MELIDE (SE, Playa de Melide ~42 23.4N 8 55.5W). Approach from E via Canal de Ons between Onza islet and Ons. ~60-70 anchorages/day total - PERMIT REQUIRED. Stay clear of swimmer-buoy exclusion zones (200 m off shore).
swellSensitivity: moderate - W/SW swell at Melide; Castelo more sheltered
nearestTown: No services except a small bar near Almacen village; Sanxenxo 5 nm.
orcaNotes: Offshore W of Ons is active orca zone; inside-ria passage to Sanxenxo low.
notes: National Park - PERMIT REQUIRED: (1) navigation permit by email/post, (2) anchoring permit online for dates. 60-70 places/day - book early.

name: Puerto Deportivo de Combarro (Marina Combarro)
approachNotes: Upper Ria de Pontevedra, N of Pontevedra city. Outer pontoon 3 m MLW (charts often show shallower - marina dredged). Approach from S mid-channel; old chart depths misleading. Anchor off outer pontoon in ~6 m at HW if full.
bestTideEntry: outer pontoon 3 m MLW - sufficient for most cruisers
nearestTown: Combarro (adjacent) - one of Galicia's most beautiful villages (granite horreos, rock-carved restaurants); Pontevedra 5 km.
orcaNotes: Fully inside ria.
notes: 5-star marina, 5 TYHA Gold Anchors - one of Spain's finest small marinas. 50 t travelift. Highly recommended.

name: Pontevedra / Club Naval de Pontevedra
approachNotes: Mouth of the Rio Lerez at the head of the ria. Severely limited: max draft 2 m at LW (spring range ~3 m); Barca bridge blocks sailing vessels over 12 m air draft. Not suitable for most offshore cruising yachts.
nearestTown: Pontevedra old city (immediate) - fine medieval city, hospitals, rail station.
orcaNotes: Fully inland.
notes: Impractical for most cruising yachts (bridge air draft + shallow LW). Combarro is a far better option; visit Pontevedra by dinghy/bus.
verifyNeeded: all technical details - bridge air draft vs mast height critical

name: Marin - Port of Marin
approachNotes: Large mixed military/commercial/fishing port at the head of Ria de Pontevedra (Spanish Naval Academy). Not a recreational marina - contact port authority VHF 16; temporary fishing-quay berth not routine.
nearestTown: Marin (immediate); Pontevedra 5 km.
orcaNotes: Fully inside ria.
notes: NOT a recreational marina. Navy/commercial/fishing. Use Combarro or Sanxenxo instead.

name: Puerto Pesquero y Deportivo de Bueu
approachNotes: S shore of Ria de Pontevedra at the entrance to Ria de Aldan. Approach from main channel - straightforward. Busy mussel fleet; sports area separate from fishing quays.
swellSensitivity: low-moderate; some SW swell at mouth
nearestTown: Bueu (adjacent) - active fishing village, fish market; Pontevedra 15 km.
orcaNotes: Inside ria; offshore Cabo Udra at ria entrance medium.
notes: Useful stop between Pontevedra/Sanxenxo and Ria de Vigo; gateway to Ria de Aldan. Limited capacity (100 berths).

name: Ria de Aldan - anchorage
approachNotes: Narrow inlet on the Morrazo Peninsula between Punta Couso (O Hio) and Cabo Udra (Beluso). Enter from NW; clean water in the gut. Anchor mid-ria in 4-8 m sand. Small fishing/sports port at Aldan village.
nearestTown: Aldan village (restaurants); Cangas/Bueu 8 km.
orcaNotes: Inside sheltered inlet negligible; Cabo Udra offshore medium.
notes: One of the most beloved Galician anchorages - warm, calm, crystal-clear water; warmest bathing in the Rias Baixas. A must-stop.

name: Porto Deportivo de Cangas (RCN Rodeira)
approachNotes: N shore of Ria de Vigo between Cabo Balea and Punta Rodeira. Day: enter S (main) channel on Cabo Stay leading line 069; then 041 on Borneira tower/Rodeira/Domaio; leave red Borneira buoy to port; NE to breakwater. Night: Cies then Cabo Silleiro; Boeiro buoy; 069 then 040.
swellSensitivity: low inside ria; entry channel exposed to occasional SW
nearestTown: Cangas (adjacent) - fishing town, market; ferry to Vigo (25 min).
orcaNotes: Inside Ria de Vigo low-medium (orcas now to <20 m contour in 2025); Cies entrance / S channel higher.
notes: Excellent well-run marina; 64 t travelift; ferry to Vigo for crew changes; access to Cies (7 nm).

name: Puerto Deportivo Moana Mar
approachNotes: N shore of Ria de Vigo, E of Cangas. From mid-channel, when Vigo Maritime Station bears ahead (S) steer for the Moana breakwater tip. Fishing port to W, sports marina to E.
nearestTown: Moana (adjacent) - 4 supermarkets incl. Carrefour, chandlery, launderette; ferry to Vigo (30 min).
orcaNotes: Inside ria; orca reported in the ria summer 2025 - keep watch.
notes: Quiet town-centre marina; good value (~EUR20/night to 12 m); very well protected.

name: Ensenada de Barra - anchorage
approachNotes: N shore of Ria de Vigo, ~3 nm NE of Islas Cies. Wide bay (1 nm) with 3 beaches. Best anchorage off the WESTERNMOST beach - most sheltered from prevailing NW swell. Good holding muddy sand. Uncomfortable above moderate W/NW.
swellSensitivity: moderate - NW swell enters ria; W beach best protection
nearestTown: Small hamlet (one bar); Moana 2 km; ferry to Vigo.
orcaNotes: Inside ria low; orca penetrated to <20 m contour near entrance in 2025.
notes: Popular raft-up anchorage with dunes/pine woodland; ~10 nm from Aldan (natural day-sail pairing).

name: Islas Cies - anchorage (Ensenada de Rodas)
approachNotes: Three islands at the mouth of Ria de Vigo. Main anchorage Ensenada de Rodas (E side, inside the Rodas-beach tombolo). Approach from E via Canal Sur (S of Cies, well marked; from Cabo Stay 069 then between Punta Vicos and S tip of Cies on 032). Anchor 3-5 m sand inside the lagoon. 75-125 anchorages/day across zones.
swellSensitivity: low inside Rodas lagoon; San Martino / outer E face more exposed
nearestTown: No facilities; high-season ferry to Vigo; ranger station on Monteagudo.
orcaNotes: CRITICAL - Cies sit in the main orca interaction zone at the mouth of Ria de Vigo. 2024-25 interactions in the Vigo estuary itself (German yacht lost rudder Aug 2024). Approach from INSIDE the ria to minimise exposure; stay <20 m depth; monitor GT Orcas; Fisterra Radio Ch 16.
notes: National Park - PERMIT REQUIRED (navigation + anchoring, online for dates). 75-125 places/day - books out weeks ahead in high season. Rodas beach world-renowned.

name: Real Club Nautico de Vigo
approachNotes: City-centre, S shore of Ria de Vigo. Approach from W via main channel. Mouth 28 m wide, 10 m at mouth but only 2 m at innermost berths - critical; best berths 4-8 m. 32 t travelift.
bestTideEntry: inner harbour 2 m min - check draft
nearestTown: Vigo city centre (immediate) - major city, international airport, ferries to PT/UK, all provisioning.
orcaNotes: Fully inside ria; orca reported in lower ria 2025 but upper-ria/city waterfront low.
notes: Most central of Vigo's four marinas; immediate urban amenities; indoor pool/gym. Deeper yachts better at Davila or Punta Lagoa.

name: Marina Davila Sport (Vigo / Bouzas)
approachNotes: Bouzas industrial waterfront, W end of Vigo. Deep water (20 m mouth, 18 m inner). Purpose-built for superyachts; on the repair quay. Enter from W; marked within port complex. Customs/immigration on site. VHF 09.
nearestTown: Bouzas, 10 min from Vigo centre; full services.
orcaNotes: Inside Vigo harbour.
notes: Premier Atlantic superyacht marina; 70 t travelift; full refit/repair (Astilleros Lagos nearby); customs on site (good for non-EU arrivals). High dock fees.
verifyNeeded: visitor availability for cruising yachts vs superyachts

name: Marina Punta Lagoa (Vigo / Teis)
approachNotes: E end of Ria de Vigo (Teis). From mid-ria; 4.5 m entrance, 3.5 m inner. Entrance width 20 m - narrower than other Vigo marinas; care in cross-wind. 110 t travelift (largest in the region).
nearestTown: Teis (N side of Vigo); steep hill to city services - car helpful.
orcaNotes: Fully inside ria.
notes: Best haulout in Ria de Vigo (110 t travelift); used for wintering/major repairs. Not ideal for an overnight (no waterfront restaurants nearby).

name: Liceo Maritimo de Bouzas (Vigo)
approachNotes: Bouzas, just E of Davila Sport. Approach from W. Entrance 3 m; max vessel 12 m. 8 t crane; 48 buoy berths plus pontoons. Blue Flag.
nearestTown: Bouzas, 10 min walk from Vigo centre.
orcaNotes: Inside ria.
notes: Blue Flag sailing club; small-medium yachts (max 12 m); budget-friendlier than Davila.

name: Monte Real Club de Yates de Baiona (MRCYB)
approachNotes: SW shore of Ria de Vigo, 4 nm SSW of Islas Cies. Approach from S/NW round Cabo Silleiro (keep 1+ nm off large rocks); NW with Monte Real castle as landmark; enter between breakwaters ~083 leading line; entrance 12 m, inner 6 m.
swellSensitivity: low-moderate; W/SW swell can enter in heavy weather
nearestTown: Baiona 5 min - historic town (first European port to hear of Columbus's return), Parador castle hotel, excellent restaurants, fish market.
orcaNotes: Baiona is at the ENTRANCE to Ria de Vigo - the primary Galician orca zone. Estuary interaction Aug 2024 (German yacht rudder torn off); Cabo Silleiro offshore very high. Check GT Orcas; stay <20 m; Fisterra Radio Ch 16.
notes: One of the most prestigious yacht clubs on Spain's Atlantic coast. Baiona is a top-5 Galician stop. Pinta arrived here 1493. ASNAUGA discount.
verifyNeeded: VHF 71 (differs from regional Ch 09 - confirm vs Puerto Deportivo Baiona Ch 09)

name: Puerto Deportivo de Baiona
approachNotes: Adjacent to MRCYB, same bay. Entrance ~083 (leading line with Punta Vicos on Cies and Cabo Silleiro 1.3 nm astern). Approach from SW/S. 12 m entrance, 6 m inner. VHF 09. 17 t travelift.
nearestTown: Baiona (immediate) - same as MRCYB.
orcaNotes: Same high-risk zone as MRCYB - see above.
notes: Municipal marina adjacent to MRCYB; often has space when MRCYB is full. Max 40 m / 6 m draft; fuel dock.

name: Puerto de A Guarda
approachNotes: Open cove facing NW (400x100 m). Landmark Monte Santa Tecla. Entrance alignment beacons on 109; entrance width 8.2 m - VERY NARROW. Fog signal. Deepens to 8 m at breakwater head, 0 m at slipway. Three exposed rocks off Punta Jinete (N shore). DANGEROUS in W/SW - do not attempt entry. Fishing harbour.
swellSensitivity: poor - exposed W/NW; dangerous in bad weather
nearestTown: A Guarda (adjacent) - last Galician town before Portugal; Monte Santa Tecla Celtic hillfort viewpoint over the Rio Mino.
orcaNotes: Cabo Silleiro (5 nm NW) and the Rio Mino mouth offshore are documented orca zones. Baiona->A Guarda passage HIGH risk - hug the 20 m contour.
notes: ONLY for small craft in settled weather; 8.2 m entrance is very tight; emergency use only for cruising yachts - a W blow could trap a vessel. Last port in Spain.
